req.title: Create observation (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for Observation creation requirements.

Requirement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with
LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected.
Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

Assumes a FHIR R4-compatible API backed by a service layer.
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-internal imports (adjust paths to match your actual module layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from app.auth import roles  # e.g. CLINICIAN, PATIENT, ADMIN, ANONYMOUS
from app.exceptions import (
    ConflictError,
    ForbiddenError,
    UnauthorizedError,
    UnprocessableEntityError,
    ValidationError,
)
from app.models.observation import Observation, ObservationStatus
from app.services.observation_service import ObservationService

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
VALID_LOINC_SYSTEM = "http://loinc.org"
VALID_LOINC_CODE = "85354-9"          # Blood pressure panel
VALID_LOINC_DISPLAY = "Blood pressure panel with all children optional"
PATIENT_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
PRACTITIONER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
ENCOUNTER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / Factories
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_minimal_observation(
    patient_id: str = PATIENT_ID,
    loinc_code: str = VALID_LOINC_CODE,
    loinc_system: str = VALID_LOINC_SYSTEM,
    status: str = ObservationStatus.FINAL,
) -> dict:
    """Return the smallest valid Observation payload."""
    return {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": status,
        "code": {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": loinc_system,
                    "code": loinc_code,
                    "display": VALID_LOINC_DISPLAY,
                }
            ]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


def build_observation_with_value(**overrides) -> dict:
    """Return a valid Observation payload that includes a numeric value."""
    obs = build_minimal_observation()
    obs["valueQuantity"] = {
        "value": 120,
        "unit": "mmHg",
        "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
        "code": "mm[Hg]",
    }
    obs.update(overrides)
    return obs


def clinician_context() -> dict:
    """Simulated auth context for a clinician."""
    return {"user_id": PRACTITIONER_ID, "role": roles.CLINICIAN}


def patient_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": PATIENT_ID, "role": roles.PATIENT}


def admin_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": str(uuid.uuid4()), "role": roles.ADMIN}


def anonymous_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": None, "role": roles.ANONYMOUS}


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def service(mocker):
    """
    Return an ObservationService whose external dependencies (DB, auth,
    terminology validator) are mocked so tests run without infrastructure.
    """
    svc = ObservationService(
        db=mocker.MagicMock(),
        auth_provider=mocker.MagicMock(),
        terminology_validator=mocker.MagicMock(),
    )
    # By default, LOINC validation succeeds
    svc.terminology_validator.is_valid_loinc.return_value = True
    # Patient exists by default
    svc.db.patient_exists.return_value = True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def saved_observation(service):
    """Pre-saved final Observation for state-transition tests."""
    obs = Observation(**build_minimal_observation())
    obs.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    obs.status = ObservationStatus.FINAL
    service.db.get_observation.return_value = obs
    return obs


# ===========================================================================
# POSITIVE PATHS
# ===========================================================================

class TestCreateObservationSuccess:
    """Happy-path scenarios: clinician creates valid, LOINC-coded Observations."""

    def test_create_minimal_observation_returns_created(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert result["resourceType"] == "Observation"
        assert "id" in result

    def test_created_observation_preserves_loinc_code(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        coding = result["code"]["coding"][0]
        assert coding["system"] == VALID_LOINC_SYSTEM
        assert coding["code"] == VALID_LOINC_CODE

    def test_created_observation_linked_to_patient(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation(patient_id=PATIENT_ID)
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert result["subject"]["reference"] == f"Patient/{PATIENT_ID}"

    def test_create_observation_with_value_quantity(self, service):
        payload = build_observation_with_value()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert result["valueQuantity"]["value"] == 120

    def test_create_observation_with_preliminary_status(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation(status=ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY)
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert result["status"] == ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY

    def test_create_observation_with_encounter_context(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["encounter"] = {"reference": f"Encounter/{ENCOUNTER_ID}"}
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert result["encounter"]["reference"] == f"Encounter/{ENCOUNTER_ID}"

    def test_create_persists_to_database(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        service.db.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_admin_can_also_create_observation(self, service):
        """Admins should not be blocked from creating Observations."""
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=admin_context())

        assert "id" in result


# ===========================================================================
# CODE ELEMENT VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestCodeElementValidation:
    """The code element must be present and carry a valid LOINC coding."""

    def test_missing_code_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        del payload["code"]

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="code"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_empty_code_object_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"] = {}

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="code"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_code_with_empty_coding_list_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"] = {"coding": []}

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="coding"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_code_without_loinc_system_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation(loinc_system="http://snomed.info/sct")

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="LOINC"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_code_with_unknown_loinc_code_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        service.terminology_validator.is_valid_loinc.return_value = False
        payload = build_minimal_observation(loinc_code="99999-9")

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="LOINC"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_code_with_blank_code_value_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"]["coding"][0]["code"] = ""

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="code"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_code_system_none_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"]["coding"][0]["system"] = None

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_multiple_codings_accepted_when_loinc_present(self, service):
        """Additional non-LOINC codings are allowed as long as LOINC is present."""
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"]["coding"].append(
            {
                "system": "http://snomed.info/sct",
                "code": "75367002",
                "display": "Blood pressure",
            }
        )
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        assert len(result["code"]["coding"]) == 2

    def test_loinc_terminology_validator_is_called(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

        service.terminology_validator.is_valid_loinc.assert_called_once_with(
            VALID_LOINC_CODE
        )


# ===========================================================================
# MANDATORY ELEMENT VALIDATION
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryElements:
    """Missing mandatory FHIR elements must be rejected."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["status", "subject"])
    def test_missing_top_level_mandatory_field_raises(self, service, missing_field):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        del payload[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match=missing_field):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_missing_resource_type_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        del payload["resourceType"]

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="resourceType"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_wrong_resource_type_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["resourceType"] = "Patient"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="resourceType"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_subject_without_reference_key_raises(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["subject"] = {}

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="subject"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_subject_reference_not_patient_type_raises(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["subject"]["reference"] = f"Practitioner/{PRACTITIONER_ID}"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="Patient"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_nonexistent_patient_raises_unprocessable(self, service):
        service.db.patient_exists.return_value = False
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises(UnprocessableEntityError, match="Patient"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_invalid_status_value_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["status"] = "unknown-status"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="status"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_empty_payload_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.create({}, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_none_payload_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        with pytest.raises((ValidationError, TypeError)):
            service.create(None, auth_context=clinician_context())


# ===========================================================================
# PERMISSION / AUTHORISATION DENIAL
# ===========================================================================

class TestPermissionDenial:
    """Only authorised roles may create Observations."""

    def test_anonymous_user_raises_unauthorized(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=anonymous_context())

    def test_patient_role_raises_forbidden(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises(ForbiddenError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=patient_context())

    def test_missing_auth_context_raises_unauthorized(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises((UnauthorizedError, TypeError)):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=None)

    def test_auth_context_without_role_raises_forbidden(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        bad_context = {"user_id": str(uuid.uuid4())}  # no 'role' key

        with pytest.raises((ForbiddenError, KeyError)):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=bad_context)

    def test_expired_token_context_raises_unauthorized(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        expired_context = {"user_id": PRACTITIONER_ID, "role": roles.CLINICIAN,
                           "token_expired": True}

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=expired_context)

    def test_database_save_not_called_when_unauthorized(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises(UnauthorizedError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=anonymous_context())

        service.db.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_database_save_not_called_when_forbidden(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()

        with pytest.raises(ForbiddenError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=patient_context())

        service.db.save.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# STATE TRANSITION CONFLICTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestStateTransitions:
    """
    Certain status transitions are illegal (e.g. entered-in-error -> final).
    Attempts to update a cancelled/entered-in-error Observation must be blocked.
    """

    # --- Valid transitions --------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "from_status, to_status",
        [
            (ObservationStatus.REGISTERED, ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
            (ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
            (ObservationStatus.FINAL, ObservationStatus.AMENDED),
            (ObservationStatus.AMENDED, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
            (ObservationStatus.FINAL, ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR),
        ],
    )
    def test_valid_status_transition_succeeds(
        self, service, saved_observation, from_status, to_status
    ):
        saved_observation.status = from_status
        update_payload = {"status": to_status}
        service.db.save.return_value = {
            **build_minimal_observation(status=to_status),
            "id": saved_observation.id,
        }

        result = service.update_status(
            saved_observation.id, update_payload, auth_context=clinician_context()
        )

        assert result["status"] == to_status

    # --- Invalid transitions ------------------------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "from_status, to_status",
        [
            (ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
            (ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
            (ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, ObservationStatus.AMENDED),
            (ObservationStatus.CANCELLED, ObservationStatus.FINAL),
            (ObservationStatus.CANCELLED, ObservationStatus.PRELIMINARY),
            (ObservationStatus.FINAL, ObservationStatus.REGISTERED),
        ],
    )
    def test_invalid_status_transition_raises_conflict(
        self, service, saved_observation, from_status, to_status
    ):
        saved_observation.status = from_status
        update_payload = {"status": to_status}

        with pytest.raises(ConflictError, match="transition"):
            service.update_status(
                saved_observation.id,
                update_payload,
                auth_context=clinician_context(),
            )

    def test_updating_entered_in_error_observation_raises_conflict(
        self, service, saved_observation
    ):
        saved_observation.status = ObservationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR

        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update(
                saved_observation.id,
                build_observation_with_value(),
                auth_context=clinician_context(),
            )

    def test_updating_cancelled_observation_raises_conflict(
        self, service, saved_observation
    ):
        saved_observation.status = ObservationStatus.CANCELLED

        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update(
                saved_observation.id,
                build_minimal_observation(),
                auth_context=clinician_context(),
            )

    def test_same_status_update_does_not_raise(self, service, saved_observation):
        """Idempotent status update (FINAL -> FINAL) should be accepted."""
        saved_observation.status = ObservationStatus.FINAL
        update_payload = {"status": ObservationStatus.FINAL}
        service.db.save.return_value = {
            **build_minimal_observation(status=ObservationStatus.FINAL),
            "id": saved_observation.id,
        }

        # Should not raise
        result = service.update_status(
            saved_observation.id, update_payload, auth_context=clinician_context()
        )

        assert result["status"] == ObservationStatus.FINAL

    def test_transition_on_nonexistent_observation_raises_not_found(self, service):
        service.db.get_observation.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(Exception, match="[Nn]ot [Ff]ound|404"):
            service.update_status(
                str(uuid.uuid4()),
                {"status": ObservationStatus.AMENDED},
                auth_context=clinician_context(),
            )

    def test_concurrent_update_raises_conflict(self, service, saved_observation):
        """Simulate optimistic-locking / ETag mismatch."""
        saved_observation.version = 3
        update_payload = build_minimal_observation()
        update_payload["meta"] = {"versionId": "1"}  # stale version

        with pytest.raises(ConflictError, match="[Vv]ersion|[Cc]oncurr"):
            service.update(
                saved_observation.id,
                update_payload,
                auth_context=clinician_context(),
            )


# ===========================================================================
# BOUNDARY & EDGE CASES
# ===========================================================================

class TestBoundaryAndEdgeCases:
    """Additional edge cases not covered by the main categories."""

    def test_duplicate_observation_id_raises_conflict(self, service):
        existing_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        service.db.exists.return_value = True
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["id"] = existing_id

        with pytest.raises(ConflictError, match="[Dd]uplicate|[Ee]xists"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_extra_unknown_fields_are_ignored_or_stored(self, service):
        """Unknown extensions should not cause a hard failure."""
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["unknownField"] = "some_value"
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        # Should not raise; implementation may ignore or store the extra field
        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())
        assert "id" in result

    def test_very_long_loinc_display_text_is_accepted(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["code"]["coding"][0]["display"] = "A" * 10_000
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())
        assert "id" in result

    def test_observation_for_deceased_patient_still_creates(self, service):
        """Clinical systems must retain historical data for deceased patients."""
        service.db.patient_exists.return_value = True
        service.db.patient_is_deceased.return_value = True
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())
        assert "id" in result

    def test_sql_injection_in_patient_id_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        malicious_patient_id = "1; DROP TABLE patients;--"
        payload = build_minimal_observation(patient_id=malicious_patient_id)

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_effective_date_in_future_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["effectiveDateTime"] = "2099-12-31T23:59:59Z"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="[Ff]uture|[Ee]ffective"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    def test_malformed_effective_date_raises_validation_error(self, service):
        payload = build_minimal_observation()
        payload["effectiveDateTime"] = "not-a-date"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="[Dd]ate|[Ff]ormat"):
            service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("status", list(ObservationStatus))
    def test_all_valid_statuses_accepted_on_create(self, service, status):
        """
        FHIR allows any valid status on creation; business rules may further
        restrict, but the status itself must parse without error.
        """
        payload = build_minimal_observation(status=status)
        service.db.save.return_value = {**payload, "id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        try:
            result = service.create(payload, auth_context=clinician_context())
            assert result["status"] == status
        except (ValidationError, ConflictError):
            # Business rules may prohibit some statuses on creation – that is fine
            pass
```

---

## Design Notes

| Concern | How it is addressed |
|---|---|
| **LOINC presence** | `TestCodeElementValidation` – 10 tests covering missing, empty, wrong system, blank code, invalid code, and mixed-codings |
| **Mandatory elements** | `TestMandatoryElements` – parametrised over every required FHIR field; also covers wrong `resourceType`, non-existent patient, and `None` payload |
| **Permission denial** | `TestPermissionDenial` – anonymous, patient role, `None` context, missing role key, expired token; verifies DB is never called |
| **State transitions** | `TestStateTransitions` – parametrised valid *and* invalid transitions, idempotent re-set, optimistic-lock conflict, update-on-terminal-status |
| **Boundary / edge** | Duplicate IDs, unknown fields, very long strings, deceased patients, future dates, SQL injection, every valid status variant |
| **Isolation** | All external dependencies (DB, auth, terminology) are mocked via `pytest-mock`; no real infrastructure required |
| **Extensibility** | Factories (`build_minimal_observation`, `build_observation_with_value`) make adding new tests trivial |

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for Observation creation requirements.

Requirement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with
LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected.
Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

Assumes a FHIR R4-compatible API backed by a service layer.
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from http import HTTPStatus
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch

import pytest

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project-internal imports (adjust paths to match your actual module layout)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from app.auth import roles  # e.g. CLINICIAN, PATIENT, ADMIN, ANONYMOUS
from app.exceptions import (
    ConflictError,
    ForbiddenError,
    UnauthorizedError,
    UnprocessableEntityError,
    ValidationError,
)
from app.models.observation import Observation, ObservationStatus
from app.services.observation_service import ObservationService

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
VALID_LOINC_SYSTEM = "http://loinc.org"
VALID_LOINC_CODE = "85354-9"          # Blood pressure panel
VALID_LOINC_DISPLAY = "Blood pressure panel with all children optional"
PATIENT_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
PRACTITIONER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
ENCOUNTER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / Factories
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def build_minimal_observation(
    patient_id: str = PATIENT_ID,
    loinc_code: str = VALID_LOINC_CODE,
    loinc_system: str = VALID_LOINC_SYSTEM,
    status: str = ObservationStatus.FINAL,
) -> dict:
    """Return the smallest valid Observation payload."""
    return {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": status,
        "code": {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": loinc_system,
                    "code": loinc_code,
                    "display": VALID_LOINC_DISPLAY,
                }
            ]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


def build_observation_with_value(**overrides) -> dict:
    """Return a valid Observation payload that includes a numeric value."""
    obs = build_minimal_observation()
    obs["valueQuantity"] = {
        "value": 120,
        "unit": "mmHg",
        "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
        "code": "mm[Hg]",
    }
    obs.update(overrides)
    return obs


def clinician_context() -> dict:
    """Simulated auth context for a clinician."""
    return {"user_id": PRACTITIONER_ID, "role": roles.CLINICIAN}


def patient_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": PATIENT_ID, "role": roles.PATIENT}


def admin_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": str(uuid.uuid4()), "role": roles.ADMIN}


def anonymous_context() -> dict:
    return {"user_id": None, "role": roles.ANONYMOUS}

@pytest.fixture
def service(mocker):
    """
    Return an ObservationService whose external dependencies (DB, auth,
    terminology validator) are mocked so tests run without infrastructure.
    """
    svc = ObservationService(
        db=mocker.MagicMock(),
        auth_provider=mocker.MagicMock(),
        terminology_validator=mocker.MagicMock(),
    )
    # By default, LOINC validation succeeds
    svc.terminology_validator.is_valid_loinc.return_value = True
    # Patient exists by default
    svc.db.patient_exists.return_value = True
    return svc
